title: To His Friend, Mr. J. Jincks.
content:
Love, love me now, because I place
    Thee here among my righteous race:
    The bastard slips may droop and die
    Wanting both root and earth; but thy
    Immortal self shall boldly trust
    To live for ever with my Just.
meaning:
"To His Friend, Mr. J. Jincks." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
